summary: The cross product of two 3-D vectors is a new vector perpendicular to both, with length equal to the area of the parallelogram they span. (In 2-D it collapses to the single scalar uâ‚پvâ‚‚ âˆ’ uâ‚‚vâ‚پ.)
formulas: uأ—v = (uâ‚‚vâ‚ƒ âˆ’ uâ‚ƒvâ‚‚,  uâ‚ƒvâ‚پ âˆ’ uâ‚پvâ‚ƒ,  uâ‚پvâ‚‚ âˆ’ uâ‚‚vâ‚پ)
uأ—v âٹ¥ u  and  uأ—v âٹ¥ v
method: For the first component, cover column 1 and cross-multiply: uâ‚‚vâ‚ƒ âˆ’ uâ‚ƒvâ‚‚.
For the second, cover column 2 and remember the sign flip: uâ‚ƒvâ‚پ âˆ’ uâ‚پvâ‚ƒ.
For the third, cover column 3: uâ‚پvâ‚‚ âˆ’ uâ‚‚vâ‚پ.
Check the result is perpendicular to both u and v (its dot product with each is 0).

example.title: Cross product of axis vectors
steps: Apply the formula; only the z-component survives: (0, 0, 1آ·1 âˆ’ 0آ·0).
